rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees on component ports in the component type scope; implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope.
AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by scoping AGREE contracts to the correct AADL element (type or implementation).
Use case-provided bounds and visible feature names when defining range assumptions and guarantees.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid implementation-specific paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree { ... };` to enclose AGREE contracts.
Define boolean range checks with `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly in AGREE expressions without corresponding ports or constants.
Avoid referencing subcomponent paths within component type annexes.
